channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Global Game Jam launches micro-grant program to support indies and emerging talent
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

May 28, 2026

1 Min Read

Logo and photograph via Global Game Jam

Global Game Jam has partnered with Endless Foundation to launch a new micro-grant initiative called 'Small Projects, Big Impact.' 

The pilot program will distribute micro-grants worth up to $2,500 to indie studios in a bid to support emerging talent. It will connect successful applicants with aspiring game professionals (aged 18 and over) by facilitating short-form, paid, micro-projects across a range of disciplines. 

The aim is twofold: help indie studios accelerate development by offering micro-project support while providing emerging developers with invaluable work experience.

"The pilot will bring together indie game studios and members of the Global Game Jam community through small, focused tasks that can be completed in under 48 hours. Participants will gain real-world experience, portfolio material, and paid opportunities, while studios will receive original creative assets and valuable QA support," reads an explainer on the Global Game Jam website. 

Related:Valve raises Steam Deck OLED prices by over $200

"Over the next year, Global Game Jam will select indie studios to receive microgrants of up to $2,500 USD each. Studios will work with GGJ to create micro-projects supporting their active game development efforts. Participants can review available micro-projects posted by studios, submit their work or sign up for assignments, and receive payment upon selection or completion."

Global Game Jam will coordinate participant onboarding, payment management, and project administration. 

The initiative is expected to support at least 25 indie studios and engage more than 500 participants globally. Global Game Jam hopes it will generate between 1,500 and 2,000 paid micro-projects during its first year.

For more information on the program visit the Global Game Jam website.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/global-game-jam-launches-micro-grant-program-to-support-indies-and-emerging-talent
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Valve raises Steam Deck OLED prices by over $200
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

May 28, 2026

1 Min Read

Valve has raised Steam Deck OLED prices by over $200 in the United States.

The company has also increased prices in other key markets such as Canada, Europe, Australia, and the United Kingdom.

The Steam Deck OLED 512GB model will now cost $789 in the U.S., having previously retailed for $549. The 1TB console will now cost $949, a significant upswing on its previous retail price of $649. 

Valve said it has been forced to raise the price of hardware "due to rising memory and storage costs." 

"Steam Deck itself hasn't changed; these new prices reflect the current state of component costs and other global logistical challenges across the industry as a whole. We’ll keep you updated if anything changes," added the company. 

Here are the new Steam Deck OLED prices (inclusive of VAT where applicable): 

  • Steam Deck OLED 512GB: Now $789 USD; CAD 1,129; EUR 779; GBP 649; AUD 1,199; PLN 3,279

  • Steam Deck OLED 1TB: Now $949 USD; CAD 1,349; EUR 919; GBP 779; AUD 1,429; PLN 3,879

Related:Report: Payment for some TheGamer staff now tied to per-article sessions

Other major console manufacturers including Sony, #Microsoft, and Nintendo have implemented similar price hikes as the global electronics industry struggles to contend with a memory crisis driven by rampant investment in artificial intelligence (thanks, Bloomberg).

As noted by Nintendo president Shuntaro Furukawa, the US-Israeli war on Iran and resulting surge in oil prices is also impacting hardware profitably and by extension pricing decisions. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/valve-raises-steam-deck-oled-prices-by-over-200
